Jowee Omicil casts a spell over the Jazz à Porquerolles festival with a spiritual and festive maelstrom combining jazz, gospel, Afro-Haitian rhythms and hip-hop.

Film_title: Scottish National Youth Orchestra @ Young Euro Classic 2025
Description: The Scottish National Youth Orchestra performs Jay Capperauld's accordion concerto. In this new work, the Glasgow composer imposes spectacular and acrobatic figures on the ‘piano with braces’. In the second half, the audience is treated to Berlioz's Symphonie fantastique, featuring an ‘episode in the life of an artist’. Accordionist Ryan Corbett plays the solo part alongside the Scottish musicians and their conductor Catherine Larsen-Maguire.

Toxic waste has been illegally dumped into the local lake, and now mutilated bodies are turning up covered in sludge. As the police investigate, a team of scientists joins the case. Dr. Dawn suspects that exposure to the chemicals has caused a terrifying mutation. The authorities must act fast to uncover what kind of creature is behind the attacks before more people are killed and the lake becomes a breeding ground for terror.

After a night of drinking, friends Charlie and Tom take a rideshare home—but their driver abducts them, delivering them to a grim warehouse where victims are tortured for entertainment. Strapped to a chair, Charlie becomes the unwilling star of a sadistic underground show. When Tom awakens, he finds his friend mutilated and a line of masked men waiting their turn.
Recording of the 2025 run of the Pirates of Penzance by the Gilbert & Sullivan Opera Company in Chicago
Studios: The Gilbert and Sullivan Opera Company

Director: Tope Adebayo Salami, Adebayo Tijani et al
Description: When a tyrant bound to a bloodthirsty spirit tightens his grip on the land through slavery, a fearless challenger rises, unleashing a storm of blades, betrayal, and supernatural fury in the fight to reclaim freedom and destiny.

Film_title: The Songs of Joni Mitchell - with Yaël Naïm, Charlie Winston, Delgrés…
Description: Kate Stables, Jesca Hoop, and Lail Arad have assembled an exceptional ensemble to reinterpret Joni Mitchell’s repertoire on stage at the Casino de Paris. More than just a concert, this evening celebrates the songwriting and activism of a major figure in folk music.
Young Willow discovers her magical heritage and is transported to Wickensburg in the 1600s, where she must stop Mr. Hexenmeister from seizing an ancient artifact.
A woman grapples with alopecia and self-worth in a world obsessed with beauty, discovering her true identity and confidence through faith.
Live performance from Iamthemorning, with solo sets from Marjana Semkina and Gleb Kolyadin filmed by Crystal Spotlight in the St. Matthias Church in London.
Short film created as part of the 48 Hour Film Project.

Kevin escapes to the countryside to meet Joanna on his final day in the country. Their convergence is placed at the epitome of their lives. With so much at stake, they seek solace and answers. Only out in nature can they face each other, their fears for their future - and burst into an overdue liberation.

Film_title: Zaïde, or The Path of Light  @ Salzburg Festival 2025
Description: The Pygmalion Ensemble, led by conductor and countertenor Raphaël Pichon, is renowned for its refined sound on period instruments. Together with vocal stars Sabine Devieilhe, Lea Desandre, Johannes Martin Kränzle, Julian Prégardien, and Daniel Behle, the ensemble delves into rarely performed works by Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart.
Zedd joins forces with Knock2 to deliver a high-impact live set at Niteharts Festival 2025, captured through multi-camera coverage and immersive festival production.

Film_title: Romanian National Youth Orchestra @ Young Euro Classic 2025
Description: For its opening concert, the Young Euro Classic features Austrian conductor Katharina Wincor and Romanian violinist Ioana Cristina Goicea. The two women will be joined on stage by the Romanian Youth Orchestra, who have already played a starring role at the Berlin festival.

An artist who advances their career while sacrificing their passions is haunted in their sleep by ominous knocking. After achieving this coveted success, only to find it meaningless, they now must confront this daunting realization and attempt to break the vicious cycle during an epiphanic dream.
Henry and Angel Henderson discover a treasure with a letter promising millions from dealer Mary. Billy attempts to steal it, leading the brothers on a quest to protect their newfound fortune.

Film_title: Robert Schumann : Le paradis et la péri @ La Seine musicale
Description: Condemned to wander between earth and heaven, a peri, daughter of a fallen angel and a mortal, embarks on a long journey to the East to earn her entry into paradise... Insula orchestra, conducted by Laurence Equilbey, and Accentus, led by choir director Albert Horne, bring Robert Schumann's romantic oratorio to life.
A saga through the difficulties of saying goodbye, venturing across experiences and emotions as time ticks on towards the end.

'Erased by Putin' shows the Federal Security Service (FSB) stalking a long-time activist and organizer of St. Petersburg Pride; the arrest and dismissal of a lesbian architect in Moscow; the escape of a drag artist detained in one of the many raids ordered by the Kremlin; and the loneliness and anguish of a young agender person who began their transition in secret 20 months ago in Putin's Russia, despite it being prohibited.

Enjoy a thrilling operatic double bill — Ravel's L’Heure espagnole, performed during the year of Ravel's 150th birthday anniversary, and Puccini's Gianni Schicchi — by award-winning duo Moshe Leiser and Patrice Caurier at the magnificent Palau de les Arts Reina Sofía, Valencia! The star-studded cast features renowned mezzo-soprano Eve-Maud Hubeaux and soprano Marina Monzó, as well as acclaimed baritone Ambrogio Maestri and tenor Iván Ayón Rivas, performing under the masterful baton of Michele Spotti.
Randall and Dirk, two ghost hunters, investigate the mysterious explosion of Clark Lickerman in his home. Using their ghost-hunting gear, chaos ensues as they try to summon Clark’s spirit.
A man is anonymously mailed a box with a living doll inside, slowly starting to annoy him to try to get rid of it any way possible.

Film_title: Love, Links, Archives: Gems from the Wendy Clarke Collection
Description: Wendy Clarke presents a vibrant program of her groundbreaking video art, curated from her collection at the Wisconsin Center for Film and Theater Research. Clarke’s participatory videos reflect a massive range of human experience and feeling, from tear-jerking reflections on modern romance, to teenagers video chatting in the dawn of the internet age, and a friendship documented through video correspondence in One on One. With rarely seen tapes recorded at locations including the World Trade Center, Los Angeles high schools, and a men’s prison in Chino, California, Clarke’s projects intimately document diverse perspectives on American life and culture from the 1970s through the 1990s.

Film_title: The Marley Brothers: The Legacy Concert
Description: Join Bob Marley's sons for a concert tribute in honor of their father’s 80th birthday in 2025. (57m 16s)

A concert film for LIVEwires greatest hits! It was a pretty psychedelic scene: hundreds of colored lights, a disco ball, and two projectors shining onto the band--one projecting colored slides, the other showing Ben's 2025 experimental film "Twin Reverse Blowpop."

Film_title: Psylibrium: A collection of Short Films by Sergiy Pudich
Description: A collection of eight short films by director Sergiy Pudich, shot across four countries, blending horror, drama, and dark comedy into a twisted anthology where the fragile balance between reality and imagination constantly unravels
Embark on an unforgettable journey through the rugged and untamed Appalachian mountains
When her room threatens to overflow from a leaky roof, Rainn must learn to embrace and live with the water she is trying so hard to ignore.
A warm and surreal, comic exploration of reproductive anxiety and the pressure to have it all figured out.

Film_title: The Girl From The Village: A Story Of Hope
Description: Join us on a journey of educational hope for students in poverty-stricken Kenya. Surging Films teams up with The Weiss Scholarship Foundation to bring the stories of various Kenyan scholarship recipients, hopefuls, and alumni to the forefront. In particular, we follow Christine Akoth's journey as she travels from Kenya to America in hopes to fulfill an educational journey at Elmhurst University. Getting their start in Dago, Kenya, The Weiss Scholarship Foundation’s mission is to empower children with the gift of education while working to build a world where every child gets a great education.
A Story Driven Musical Saga Directed & Produced by modus

‘Screwed Up’ was filmed at The Theatre Royal, East London in Jan 2025. In his critically acclaimed debut Special (‘No show has made me laugh more’ The Times), Simon rips into celebrity culture, social media, the police, Putin, Prince Andrew and God. Nothing is off limits, including his own mental health, his family and his five arrests. The stand-up comedy tour sold out 170 dates including three nights at London’s iconic Hammersmith Apollo.

Heir to the great voices of jazz, Dianne Reeves is renowned for her vocal virtuosity and improvisational skills. With Beautiful Life, her latest studio album to date, the singer won her fifth Grammy Award. The American also holds two honorary doctorates from Berklee College of Music and The Juilliard School.

In 1992, his first album Blufunk Is a Fact propelled him onto the international scene, carried by the global hit ‘Rhythm Is Love’. Over the course of his six studio albums, he added memorable tracks such as ‘Where Is Life’, ‘A Million Miles From Home’ and ‘Beautiful Emilie’ to his repertoire.

Get set to journey to Malta, a place where the ancient and modern world come together at work and at play. Consisting of three small islands, where beauty abounds - in life, song and scenery - its history is as deep as its natural riches, all waiting to be revealed.
A South African woman working in Nigeria is in the crossfire at the heat of a Xenophobic Attack on a Nigerian business man in South Africa.

A group of trigger-happy thugs from the streets of Detroit team up to find $1 million buried in the woods, but they only have 4 hours before a killer drug kingpin is released from prison and comes for the money. Once the thugs begin looking for the money, uninvited guests appear; then all hell breaks loose.

Step into the shadowed world of Mr. Wyatt, your unsettling guide through eight shark-fueled nightmares where sleep offers no escape. Beneath the waves, terror takes new form as dreams turn violent and every ripple hides something hungry. Each story drags you deeper into the dark where obsession festers, sanity dissolves, and the ocean itself becomes a living nightmare. Teeth slice through silence, tides pull victims toward madness, and the line between dream and drowning fades with every scream. Killable Nightmares Vol. 2: Shark Mania plunges viewers into a fever dream of aquatic horror, guided by the sinister charm of Mr. Wyatt. This is not the ocean you know. It is a descent into cold depths where the current never stops and once the water closes overhead, the dream does not end.

Jim Reed, an insurance executive who is cheating with the wife of a struggling artist, plots to end their marriage. However, Jim is struggling with his own powerful wife who suspects cheating, and she's dangerous.
When Phoebe goes on a camping trip with her best friend Kate, Something horrible happens and changes everything, leaving Phoebe and Kate questioning what's going on.
In a world far gone, a lost soul enjoys a luxurious dinner.

In the Middle Ages, Meggy, a young woman numbed by routine brutality, finds her worldview challenged when her closest friend - the heavily pregnant and quietly radical Eda - dares to dream of something better.
Empty interior landscapes over the course of a single day.

The documentary follows the story of Iryna's family (name changed), who fled Russian occupation to save her son from possible conscription. Before leaving, representatives of the occupation authorities pressured her to enroll her children in a local school, threatening to send them to an orphanage if she didn't. Another key figure in the investigation, Oksana (name changed), was promised a holiday in Crimea on the Black Sea coast by the occupying authorities. Instead, she was taken to a military training camp in Russia — part of a program established under Putin's initiative after 2022. At the camp, Oksana was taught to dig trenches, operate drones, and carry out tasks under the supervision of Russian military instructors, who in some cases fought in Russia's war against Ukraine.

Film_title: Jonathan Bree: Live in Mexico City
Description: Captured during his 18 country, 52 date Pre-Code Hollywood world tour of 2023, this document arrives not as a slick souvenir via expensive rigs or polished crews, but as a séance. Shot on vintage cameras of a pre-internet era, the image breathes with the artifacts of another time — streaked, unstable, alive. Intercut with fragments of phone footage shared by Bree’s fanatics scattered through the dark of the crowd, it gains a visceral, immersive edge.
When a murderer named Killer DeWayne comes into a cop's life only to destroy her because of a shooting of his brother things get intense and she has to fight back after her sister is kidnapped.
A forensic photographer hunts down an artistic serial killer despite facing his own demise.
A narrative short film.
Mark Normand attemps to remove a splinter from Joe List's foot. Will he succeed?

The lives of Mahogany Andrews and those around her are drastically changed after devastating news changes relationships and reveals dark secrets from the past that will have a major impact on everyone's future.
Detective Ellery Queen sets out to solve a puzzling case following the sudden death of a wealthy businessman.
A quiet woman's obsession with an influencer reawakens her own buried demons, dragging them both into a real-world nightmare of deadly consequences.

Film_title: Harnoncourt performs Monteverdi's “L'Orfeo” - Opera Great moments in music
Description: In Zurich, conductor Nikolas Harnoncourt realizes his vision of reviving the work of opera pioneer Claudio Monteverdi using historical performance practices, brilliantly staged by Jean-Pierre Ponnelle. The Monteverdi cycle brings the composer and his operas back into the public consciousness. In 1978, L’Orfeo is recorded on film.

In this workplace comedy, Aaron, an easygoing intern, and Jayden, his arrogant and overly competitive coworker, are wrapping up their final week at a marketing firm. With only one full-time position up for grabs, tension between the two escalates. When Aaron is tasked with getting the boss's birthday cake, Jayden seizes the opportunity to sabotage him. Their fierce rivalry spirals into a series of chaotic and hilarious office mishaps as both interns scramble to outdo each other in a last-ditch effort to secure the job.

Film_title: Gravity Bach Complete performance of works for organ - Vol. 4
Description: An exceptional event took place from 31 October to 1 November 2025 in St Thomas Church in Leipzig, where Bach was cantor: Johannes Lang performed the complete organ works of his illustrious predecessor. The 22-hour programme followed the liturgical year. This fourth instalment features Clavier-Übung III and chorales in praise of God.
Four Black women bond over wine, laughter, and shared anxieties about America, only to uncover deeper truths that will change them forever.
Two friends' journey through the English countryside as they reflect on memory, meaning, and the quiet beauty of ordinary moments.
Fresh out of surgery, with a head full of painkillers and a jar full of wisdom teeth, a girl too stubborn to ask for help runs into trouble on her way home.

Professor Toad, a timid and overthinking scholar, is hopelessly in love with his charismatic best friend, Captain T. Ode. Every time he tries to confess his feelings, fear and self-doubt get the better of him, leading to awkward failures and growing emotional turmoil.
Characters play the game of life with varying results.
Light and Sawdust, a brand new interview with Texas Chainsaw Massacre: The Beginning director of photography Lukas Ettlin

Film_title: President Connolly: How the Race Was Won
Description: Documentary featuring unprecedented access to the presidential election, with exclusive behind the scenes footage, interviews and fresh insight from inside and outside the campaign
When two teens make a new friend at the park, you never really know what someone has been through until you experience it yourself.

Film_title: Il Volo. Najnowszy koncert z Doliny Świątyń
Description: See a great show from three talented young Italians! They'll sing not only the most famous opera arias ("Nessun dorma," "E lucevan le stelle," "La donna è mobile"), but also hits from the film "Gladiator" and the musicals "The Phantom of the Opera" and "Cats."
A man desperate to disappear must embrace the very past he's running from when a relentless genetically enhanced killer hunts him down.
plot unknown at the time

Film_title: Naseer Shamma @ The Journey of Instruments
Description: Naseer Shamma invites the audience to the heart of Berlin, in the Pierre Boulez Auditorium. In this setting where arts and cultures meet, the Iraqi musician and his ensemble offer a musical journey in the footsteps of the oud, one of the oldest string instruments in the world.
Yumeshiro Aya music salon

Experience this groundbreaking family favourite like never before! Led by conductor Nicholas Buc, Queensland Symphony Orchestra performs the soundtrack of Disney and Pixar’s Toy Story (1995) live-to-screen in QPAC’s Concert Hall, bringing Woody, Buzz and all of your favourite characters to life. Disney and Pixar’s Toy Story in Concert features a complete screening of the film, accompanied live by full orchestra performing Oscar® and Grammy®-winning composer Randy Newman’s iconic score, including the hit song “You’ve Got a Friend in Me”. Beloved by audiences of all ages, Disney and Pixar’s Toy Story has captivated generations with its unforgettable characters, extraordinary storytelling, stunning visuals and timeless music. Make the most of the April holidays with a big-screen family event that brings together cinema, live music and one of Disney and Pixar’s most-loved stories on the Concert Hall stage.
Recorded June 26th at Mic Drop Mania in Chandler, AZ

After relocating to Syracuse, Royal and Greezy pose as modest business owners to stay under the radar. But when they meet Jaz and Audionna-two affluent women hiding the same secret-a shared past resurfaces, threatening their lives.
Bound by an unfulfilled promise, a man prepares a meal for his mother, only to find himself lost in a night where memory and reality dissolve into one.

In 1998, two Austrian DJs released a compilation of remixes of popular artists (Madonna, Depeche Mode, Roni Size, etc.) and captured the essence of the era through delicate samples, smooth trip-hop rhythms, and ethereal soundscapes. It was an immediate success. Selling over a million copies, The K&D Sessions has since been considered one of the best electronic music albums. Twenty-five years later, the Austrian duo are touring and performing a live version of this cult album.
The tale of one man's desperate attempts to leave Nigeria by any means necessary.

Through the eyes of a farmer's daughter, the land bears witness to generations of struggle and sacrifice, as she grapples with the choice to embrace the dust they call their own or chase a future beyond the fields.

A young woman discovers a shocking betrothal arranged by her late parents to settle debts. Caught between a suitor she was promised to and a manipulative lover who lied to her, she must unravel a web of lies and make a life-altering choice.

In the unsettling psychological thriller Face Time, Jane struggles to survive a nightmarish existence ruled by her mother’s unpredictable behavior and eerie, ever-changing personas. Her mother cycles through five distinct "Faces"—from the violent fury of Second Face to the haunting chaos of Third Face—each mask bringing its own terror. With only her stitched-up stuffed rabbit, Pozzo, as a confidant, Jane teeters on the edge of sanity, trapped in a house of escalating madness. Face Time blurs the line between reality and delusion in a harrowing exploration of familial bonds and fractured psyches, leaving audiences questioning the truth behind Jane's world.

When an unstable young actress auditions for the role of Hamlet and is cast as Ophelia instead, she is made to watch as a man squanders her dream role, forcing her to take arms against a sea of troubles.

In this gripping story, a young girl falls deeply in love with a man named Raymond, believing in his sincerity and good intentions. However, as their relationship develops, she begins to uncover hidden motives beneath Raymond's charm. With compelling performances and an engaging storyline, this film invites viewers to reflect on the true nature of love and the importance of understanding God’s plan in our relationships. Will the girl uncover Raymond’s hidden truths in time? Can love survive when trust is shaken?

Caught between two worlds, Rose and Jacob, mixed Isaan-Thai teens, distance themselves from their Isaan-Thai heritage. However, attending a traditional ceremony forces them to embrace their cultural identity. As they witness the beauty and significance of the rituals, they discover a sense of belonging they never knew they craved.

Gathered at the foot of the rock where Siegfried and Brünnhilde have come together, three Norns weave the fate of the world. Past, present and future are interwoven in their rope; when it breaks unexpectedly, the fates still have one oracle: the twilight of the gods is imminent! Götterdämmerung has more than one ending: Siegfried falls victim to intrigue and to his own innocence, Valhalla goes up in flames, Brünnhilde sacrifices herself to return the ring to the Rhinemaidens. Alliances are forged and immediately broken again, magical potions and helmets create painful confusions, and generational traumas result in general destruction. Everything familiar to humans and gods collapses.
When floods and walkers destroy New Orleans, a mysterious tourist teams up with a radio voice to unearth the motherload of all supplies.
Details and life of Pomorska street in Bydgoszcz
A woman's online date leads to her being strapped to a car with a bomb and forced to run dangerous errands for a mysterious voice on a walkie-talkie.
A young, unemployed man, overwhelmed by city life and his own internal struggles, takes to the streets of London to escape.

In a train carriage, Jeanne reads. A fellow passenger, despite her wishes, tries to engage her in conversation. Gradually, the other men in the compartment join in the unwanted exchange, complimenting and oppressing her. Trapped, Jeanne must find a way to rid herself of her aggressors. She grabs an empty bottle... Will she finally find peace?

A bioengineered soldier is sent to execute an aging war criminal, only to discover that they both have a past together that forces him to question his identity, his mission, and the humanity he was never meant to possess.
Friends ride through Rataje district in Poznań, exceeding time in Google Street Views
Pauli thinks he’s a Goldfish, but nobody believes him. When his sister Eva gives him an ultimatum, Pauli embarks on a quest to prove he is what he claims to be, and that he is not alone.

Film_title: Ali Ghamsari & Ensemble - The Journey of Instruments
Description: The concert by Ali Ghamsari & Ensemble showcases the târ, a long-necked Iranian lute. With its “The Journey of Instruments” series, Berlin's Pierre Boulez Hall offers a fascinating musical journey tracing the history of the oud, one of the oldest stringed instruments.

Film_title: Simon Rattle and Magdalena Kožená Rudolfinum, Prague
Description: Mezzo-soprano Magdalena Kožená joins the Czech Philharmonic Orchestra, conducted by Sir Simon Rattle, to perform Gustav Mahler's Symphony No. 4 in Prague.

Film_title: Pavarotti: The Lost Concert - Live at Llangollen 1995
Description: Marking the 40th anniversary of winning a singing competition as part of a choir from his hometown of Modena in 1955, Luciano Pavarotti keeps a long-held promise and returns to the small town in North Wales to give a very special gala concert at the 1995 Llangollen International Musical Eisteddfod. Joining Pavarotti that evening is the BBC Philharmonic Orchestra, conducted by Leone Magiera, the modern day Corale Gioachino Rossini, Modena, directed by Paolo Rossini, and, for the finale, Japanese soprano Atzuko Kawahara. The program combines popular operatic arias and choruses, Neapolitan songs, one of Pavarotti’s own compositions, orchestral overtures and interludes, and a very special duet for the finale.
Based to a true story , win 4 oscars
In the heart of the African wild, one creature reigns supreme in audacity and grit: the honey badger. Learn how these formidable animals gain the incredible toughness that has made them legendary.
On a wildly unpredictable Christmas Eve, Scrooge is thrust into a nightmarish timeline by ghostly forces that show him a life shaped by every misdeed.

In 1973, Steve Lamb was busted smuggling more than nine tons of marijuana into the sleepy fishing village of Steinhatchee, Florida. At the time, it was the biggest pot seizure in U.S. history. It changed Steve's life and his community-giving rise to an industry of fishermen-turned-pot-smugglers in his hometown of St. Pete Beach, FL and making him a local legend. 50 years later, he refuses to let that legend fade. As the sun sets on Steve Lamb, "The Green Flash" offers an intimate look at an aging pirate's attempt to leave a worthwhile legacy behind.
When an introspective Count searching for his missing brother comes across his deranged elder sibling instead, their conflict grows inevitable.
The beauty and boredom of the mundane day to day of life.
A short documentary that offers a glimpse into the creation of Lance Cardinal's piece, a miniature recreation of the residential school St. Martin's Catholic Mission School.

Film_title: The King-Seer of the Non-Existent and His God's Fool Son
Description: The King-Seer of the Non-Existent and His God's Fool Son is a performative film unfolding between theatre, ritual, and dream — a vision of power, sacrifice, and fate. From the queen’s betrayal with an idol-deity is born a fool-son — a child of alien will, a mockery of his father. The King, consumed by jealousy and dread, devours his queen and weds the very one who should have been his heir. Yet their union is doomed: fate is relentless, and the son kills the father not for the throne, but as the fulfillment of an ancient decree. Thus the kingdom dissolves, vanishing into the void, where power and sacrifice are eternal.
A lost soul is guided by a familiar presence.

Film_title: Russell Hicks: Happy to Be Here
Description: Russell Hicks heads out on his first tour, but not his first rodeo. (Warning: This is a stand up show. NOT a rodeo.) Russell Hicks is here to stay and he really is happy to be here. So, why don’t British People believe him?

West Texas is romantically truthful. In the town of Laundromat, Texas, Henry, a drifter, seeking nothing but to disappear, finds that he has more in common with its occupants and their dirty laundry than he ever could imagine.

The daughter of Dee Dee Bridgewater, soul singer China Moses is renowned for collaborating with artists such as Guru, DJ Mehdi and Meshell Ndegeocello. In France, she is also known as a presenter for MTV and TSF Jazz.

● *Arshia Seifi was born on September 3, 2003, in Tehran, Iran. He is an industrial engineer, researcher, fashion model, internet personality, social media influencer, creative content producer, and professional volleyball player.* ● *He has gained several thousand followers on social media and maintains a very active presence online. He speaks Persian and is fluent in English.*

To save her mother's life, Telema finds herself in a living nightmare that pushes her to a breaking point that changes the lives of everyone involved. The mental health impacts of gender based violence shape the world of The Breakpoint.
The story of the first journalist on OnlyFans.
We focus on two intertwined stories on the Rochester campus as a thriller and a romance unfold over the course of a week.
Studios: University of Rochester Film and Media Studies

Film_title: Ehrlich Brothers – Fascination ✨ LIVE from Wembley Arena!
Description: Experience the magic! ✨ Watch the Ehrlich Brothers’ spectacular live show Fascination, recorded at the iconic Wembley Arena in London. From breathtaking illusions to world-class magic, Andreas and Chris Ehrlich take you on a journey full of wonder, amazement, and pure excitement.
An eclectic cast of locals navigate life in Central Florida, engaging in spontaneous, unscripted conversations that reveal the raw, unpredictable nature of human connection.

Film_title: Transition sequence for a dream or fight
Description: Sonya Lacey’s silent stop-frame animation, constructed from acid-etched copper plates, cites the cloud-like formation of scene changes used in historical animated productions. 'Transition sequence for a dream or fight' revels in this visual device that prevents us from seeing, while proposing the ongoing unknown as a hospitable, generative state." – Erin Robideaux Gleeson, curator, Leap to the Place of Two Pools, Te Pātaka Toi Adam Art Gallery. Commissioned by CIRCUIT with the support of Creative New Zealand.

Kawal and Manisha, once deeply in love, are separated due to the cunning manipulation of a third party. After many years, they unexpectedly cross paths again, only to discover that their children-unaware of their parents' past-have fallen in love with each other. As their kids' budding romance unfolds, old tensions resurface, leading to a whirlwind of drama. At its heart, the film delivers a powerful message: love knows no boundaries-whether of time, culture, or religion. It stresses the importance of parents embracing their children's love, regardless of societal norms, and reveals how, even after years apart, the bond between two souls remains unbroken. Ties That Bind is a story about second chances, healing, and the enduring power of love.

A detective secretly has a alter life being a killer, he gets assigns his own case and claims to the chief it is a lost cause, he gets away easy but stays inactive for 30 years when he makes his return...

Film_title: Cristian Măcelaru conducts Ravel With Beatrice Rana
Description: 2025 marks 150 years since the birth of Maurice Ravel. To honour the musical legacy of this immensely influential composer, Cristian Măcelaru and the Orchestre National de France join forces with Beatrice Rana.

Film_title: Klaus Florian Vogt sings Mozart, Wagner, and Strauss @ Château de Neuschwanstein 2025
Description: On an open-air stage in the castle courtyard, Klaus Florian Vogt and the Munich Symphony Orchestra present a varied program featuring works by Mozart, Wagner, and Strauss.

Film_title: Mozart: Lausanne Chamber Orchestra Ton Koopman and Julia Lezhneva
Description: When he composed his motet ‘Exsultate jubilate’ K.165, Mozart was only 17 years old. He was then at the height of his fame. He was 35 when he wrote ‘The Magic Flute’ and died shortly afterwards. Dutch conductor Ton Koopman presents a programme that traces the stages of the composer's life. Russian soprano Julia Lezhneva performs the solo parts.

A young couple move into a serene row house, eager for peace and privacy. A violent storm and unsettling forces unravel their marriage. Vasanth must uncover the truth behind these supernatural occurrences before everything falls apart.

Film_title: Renaud Capuçon und Julia Hagen spielen Brahms Rheingau Musik Festival 2025
Description: At the Rheingau Festival, violinist Renaud Capuçon, cellist Julia Hagen and the Bamberg Symphony conducted by Jakub Hrusa perform Brahms's 'Double Concerto for Violin and Cello'.

Film_title: The HARD Times and Life of Clownie Volume II
Description: The story of The Worlds Only Bonafide Hype Clown seamlessly picks up right from where Volume 1 left us confused and wondering. Questions are answered as we witness Clownie faced with and experiencing the challenges of life.

Franz Ferdinand presents a special performance at the iconic Aviva Studios in Manchester. The live show promises an intimate and thrilling show featuring tracks from their latest album, "The Human Fear," alongside classic hits that defined an era. Don't miss this exclusive event, streaming live on Prime Video and the Amazon Music UK Twitch channel.

Film_title: Centenary of Pierre Boulez  Ensemble Intercontemporain & Friends
Description: The Ensemble Intercontemporain celebrates Pierre Boulez's centenary at the Philharmonie de Paris. The concert brings together Jean-Guihen Queyras and Pierre-Laurent Aimard, also featuring work by Charlotte Bray.

Film_title: Camilla Nylund sings Sibelius Conducted by Jukka-Pekka Saraste
Description: As part of the Usedomer Musikfestival 2025, the NDR Elbphilharmonie Orchestra will perform works by Wilhelm Stenhammar and Jean Sibelius under the baton of Jukka-Pekka Saraste. The programme includes four songs by Sibelius, sung and arranged by Finnish soprano Camilla Nylund, as well as the composer's Symphony No. 6.

Latvian mezzo-soprano Elīna Garanča is one of the greatest voices of our time. Accompanied by the CHAARTS ensemble, she performs the most beautiful arias from Bizet's Carmen, including the famous Habanera, in a unique arrangement for chamber ensemble.
A Documentary on the Filmmaker Ian Rintoul
A film displaying the adventures of Harris Banks and his best friend, James Chazwick.

Following the acclaimed Laurel & Hardy: Year One and Laurel & Hardy: Year Two collections, Flicker Alley and Blackhawk Films® are proud to announce the culmination of the duo's silent years. Laurel & Hardy: Year Three brings to Blu-ray disc nine of L&H's most beloved classic shorts, newly restored and joined by a host of rare archival material that also includes several of their earliest sound films.

Film_title: The Edmonton Storyteller: The Life and Times of Tony Cashman
Description: An uplifting documentary that follows the remarkable 100-year journey of Tony Cashman, an Edmonton historian, writer, and eternal optimist. From his childhood filled with storytelling to surviving the trials of World War II, and later becoming a celebrated author, Tony’s life is a testament to the promise and hope that thrives in the city of Edmonton.

The wickedest night of the year goes global! Insane Clown Posse’s legendary Hallowicked is back—and for the first time ever, Juggalos everywhere can join the chaos live from home. Witness Violent J and Shaggy 2 Dope unleash a full-throttle performance packed with horrorcore classics, deep cuts, and surprise guests, straight from Detroit’s haunted heart. Expect Faygo showers, twisted theatrics, and all the dark carnival energy you can handle.
Short film created as part of the 48 Hour Film Project.
A businessman strikes up a conversation with a strange girl after missing his bus. She tries to revive his sense of wonder by enlisting his help to repair a cardboard-and-tinfoil spacecraft.
Follows a t4t artist couple’s poetic visual meditation on giving and receiving “flowers.” It features heartfelt interviews and original songs. Flowers Forever is a love letter to the trans community.

This is a story about a shy girl from the countryside who gets a chance to study art. There she meets a mysterious photographer and makes friends, but also enemies. She learns what real friendship is and how betrayal feels. During her adventures and dangerous moments, Saria begins to understand herself. A strange flower she finds becomes a symbol of her change. When the flower opens, her heart opens too.

A retelling of Daniel Chapters 1-3 in a world of sci-fi and fantasy. Thralldom, a tyrannical regime has conquered a magical and music-loving land called Songville. Danny, Mish, and Hanan, now exiles, rise from captives to high-ranking overseers under the Iron Master’s rule. They faithfully serve their master while secretly pledging allegiance to the true High King and the ways of Songville. However, as tensions rise and the cost of resistance grows, they must decide whether to risk their lives for the sake of faith and what they truly love— or remain silent in the face of tyranny. Are they willing to put their lives on the line for it?

Film_title: Sukh Kaur Ojla: The Aunty Years
Description: Sukh Kaur Ojla is determined to redefine what it means to be an aunty, distancing herself from the gossipy aunties of her youth. Her mission? To become the best aunty for the next generation.
When a shy American-Filipino struggles with social isolation, he turns to online gaming for escape and community, unknowingly trading real-world connection for a life controlled by the screen.
A woman named Mary wakes up in her home, in a pool of blood and with a knife in her hand. Her husband is missing and Mary has no memory of what happened.

A trio of flatmates take edibles and settle in for a relaxing afternoon. Their peace is shattered by a knock at the door, and the realisation that they'd forgotten an inspection. The girls have to think fast to clean the place up and keep the landlord happy.

A young woman enjoys a walk home alone after evening drinks with her friends. But what was meant to be a quick journey home, turns into a panicked attempt to free herself from an uncomfortable encounter.
In bustling Abidjan, Narcisse, Maurice, and Kouamé, three friends from Sakassou, see a unique opportunity for fortune when a European collector approaches them to acquire rare traditional gold.
It's about an Love and Sacrifice
Jaiye Thomas is accused of a crime he didn't commit but how can his word stand against the word of a young white woman in the UK.
A new ark is created to escape a dying world.

Film_title: MetaLove: In the Game of Love
Description: When an introverted game developer with Asperger's syndrome serendipitously meets an excitable rookie gamer in a new VR game, he gradually realizes she is burdened by a secret that could alter the course of their blossoming relationship.

After a terrible tragedy strikes across America, hotshot news anchor Lyle Rooker gets into a fierce argument with the producer of his news show, Allen Erickson, over how to accurately cover the story.

In a desperate attempt to salvage their crumbling relationships, three couples attend a healing retreat on a remote island where they're met with an unnerving visit from the natives. The tranquil setting soon takes a dark and twisted turn as unsettling events unfold.
After a mishap with last weeks footage, tensions rise on the set of THE CANDLELIGHT.

A woman struggles to navigate her present life while haunted by the mental and physical trauma of her childhood. Having endured years of abuse, she unknowingly carries the scars of the past, which damage her present relationship.
The film follows Gabriel, a young Venezuelan asylum-seeker and deliverista, working to survive in New York City.

Plot is unkown, but released.

A realistic betrayal of a vengeful high school student who has been expelled coma, returning to her school with a AR-15 to unleash her vengeful terror upon an unsuspecting school full of faculty and students.

Film_title: I Woke Up Alone on Earth
Description: Diego Alvarez is the the last man, wandering a dead mega-city after an unknown collapse. As he searches for another living soul, he starts noticing repeating events, broken memories, and glowing symbols.
A scientist obsessed with taking over the world accidentally starts a zombie apocalypse with skinny jeans. Now it's up to a time traveler from 2005 to save mankind from extinction.

When retired radio host Albert Foster accidentally intercepts a mysterious transmission, he discovers that his own voice-broadcast decades earlier-is echoing back across time. Determined to unravel the anomaly, he attempts to communicate with his younger self, but there's a catch: the connection is one-way. While Albert's voice reaches the past, his younger self remains unheard-his only responses appearing in the pages of an old journal. As Albert deciphers the cryptic messages, he realizes the stakes are far greater than he imagined. Has he been guiding his own fate all along, or is something else manipulating time itself?

Soccer captain Mondli is doing his best. But with his father away and his mother not coping at home, it's not easy. When the demands at home and of his team clash, Mondli realizes what true leadership really is.

Description: REZZ ROCKS VII - Sunday October 19, 2025 REZZ's headline performance live at Red Rocks Amphitheatre, Morrison, Colorado.

Andrea Jin humorously explores her immigrant journey from Shanghai to Canada and the U.S., while offering sharp roasts of Chicago culture and personal stories about family and identity.
A workaholic young man has his horizons broadened by an encounter with a mischievous woodland spirit.
A group of friends reunite on an island camping trip where they discover a buried curse, turning their darkest secrets against them and revealing the truth behind their haunted past.

Jammu and Kashmir is very free and our hands are out of it. When Pakistani terrorists try to grab it somehow, a military officer is beheaded and the head is taken away and the body is sent. Our Indian military has to pay for it .

This story is about four close friends, both good and bad, who face many problems after being used by others. They run away from their hometown to the city, where hardships force them to become thieves. Even though they know it's wrong, they have no other choice. At the same time, few other group is searching for a valuable diamond. One night, the four friends break into a house to steal—only to find the diamond inside. What will happen when they come face to face with the group?

'Soundtrack of Tomorrow' offers an insight into the first two years of the protagonists and Wavy Budapest's career through the joint creative process of Co Lee, Aminon and Blaize, writing 'W'. The growing-up story of the period between 2022-24 presents the ars poetica of the 3 protagonists, and how, through the power of collaboration, they become defining creators of the present of the Hungarian underground hip-hop scene, together with the Wavy collective.

Ezra is a young man navigating the complexity of family dynamics and self-acceptance. In a tense, yet intimate family Christmas gathering, Ezra decides to reveal a deeply personal truth to his parents: that he is gay. What follows is a powerful exploration of family, love, and the hope for acceptance, all unfolding within the heartwarming backdrop of the holiday season. As Ezra faces his fears of rejection and indifference, he discovers that love can come in unexpected forms. This emotional, uplifting story demonstrates that the spirit of Christmas is about more than just celebration-it's about understanding and unity.

Fazıl Say concludes the Schleswig-Holstein Music Festival at the Elbphilharmonie with the Borusan Istanbul Philharmonic Orchestra and conductor Carlo Tenan. The programme for this eclectic evening includes works by Gershwin, Ravel and Tüzün, as well as Turkish pianist Fazıl Say's Sixth Symphony, composed in 2022 under the title A Hundred-Year-Old Child.

Tru Cash is young aspiring basketball player with the potential to be drafted. Savion, Tru’s cousin, forces Tru into a situation to prove his loyalty.

Hanna, a woman facing past trauma and endless life trials. Wanting to change the misfortune that befalls her, she meets an Iban bomoh who gives her an amulet chain, supposedly to protect herself from the bad luck that often strikes her. However, behind the fortune of the amulet chain, lies a darkness that slowly takes over her soul.

For Aloe Blacc, a song is more than just a track. Both a producer and performer, he combines funk, soul and political engagement in songs such as ‘I Need a Dollar’ and ‘The Man’. He is a musician who has mastered groove and the art of making bodies and minds dance.

As their lives intertwine, these four couples learn that love is more than just romance–it requires faith, sacrifice, and sometimes, forgiveness. Their journeys are filled with emotional twists that challenge their hearts and redefine their relationships.

Description: To mark the 90th birthday of Estonian composer Arvo Pärt, a special concert featuring three of his iconic works, recorded in Görlitz, Germany.

In their music, old-school productions played with raw emotion sit alongside a fragile melancholy. As if it were nothing, Lord Of The Lost strike that subtle balance between vocals of rare harshness, raw industrial guitars and sombre beauty.

Film_title: There's Something I Want to Tell You
Description: A young, broken actor must face a decision that will change his life, with the help of a mysterious therapist.

Film_title: Bob Mould Band: Rolling Stone Beach
Description: Bob Mould, once frontman of Hüsker Dü and Sugar, has shaped guitar rock like no other. At Rolling Stone Beach 2025, he shows that energy and emotion complement each other perfectly – powerful, honest, timeless. Bob Mould is a legend of US indie rock. He already wrote music history with Hüsker Dü, later with Sugar and as a solo artist. His songs combine loud rock and sensitivity. At Rolling Stone Beach 2025, he shows that great emotions can also be transported through distorted guitars. Together with his band, Mould plays classics and new pieces with the same intensity that has distinguished him for decades.
When a magazine writer is blackmailed by a coworker for using Artificial Intelligence to write articles, he turns to the AI again, only this time to brainstorm ways to get away with murder.

In 1996, in the Chuquisaca Chaco region, a fortuitous event occurred: Unaí was kicked by his mare while changing her shoes. From then on, his life changed radically when he realized he was impotent. Family relationships were so affected that his wife left him and he began a new life in the city of Sucre. A fortuitous event determined the fate of the characters, leading them down paths of heartbreak, misery, and misfortune.
A narrative short film.
From Paris to Addis Ababa with a quick stop in Bamako and Boston: with their fifth album Danama, Parisian band Arat Kilo takes Cabaret Sauvage on a journey aboard their Ethio-Jazz ship.

Brutal mob boss Vincent "Blue Eyes" Berlusconi gives up crime after his wife Angelina discovers his activities. He secretly joins a witness protection program to expose his ex organization. When he and Angelina are targeted, chaos ensues.

Film_title: Killsplaining: The Faith of Christian Hitmen
Description: Sandra Mokena joins a Christian assassination group led by Jeff Bowman. Questioning its morality and uncovering secrets, Sandra must choose between duty and breaking free in this darkly comedic tale of faith and identity.
Some are forced to "stay," while others choose to "leave." This "goodbye" might never lead to a "reunion," but the selfless love between two children becomes the salvation for all.

A popular YouTuber from the Philippines is invited to Everest Base Camp in Nepal to find out about the beautiful Himalayan Glaciers, the origin of freshwater to billions of lives.

As division and hate rise, a street artist uses spray paint to bring about love, inclusion, and unity.

Description: In Kingston, an Iraqi refugee, Hisham, discovers a passion for art while navigating a new life.

Film_title: Der Prozess - MusikTheater an der Wien at Kammeroper Wien
Description: ‘Someone must have slandered Josef K, for one morning, without having done anything wrong, he was arrested.’ This is the famous opening line of Franz Kafka’s novel Der Prozess (The Trial). Gottfried von Einem’s opera of the same name also begins with this mysterious arrest. Josef K. suddenly finds himself in a world he cannot understand, faced with an accusation of which he knows nothing but which seems perfectly clear to everyone else and yet remains inexplicable; with an absurd court case that nevertheless follows strict rules; with an arrest that appears to have no effect on his life yet still leads to his death. Is Josef K. the victim of a conspiracy, or is his belief in a conspiracy the only chance of explaining an incomprehensible world?

Film_title: What's Black About It? Burrell: Advertising Revolution
Director: Denise Pendleton, Darryl Manuel et al
Description: A vibrant documentary that dives into Tom Burrell's revolutionary impact on the advertising industry.

With his signature deadpan delivery, Patrick Warburton tackles family, pop culture, sports, and awkward moments. Confident, charming, and uniquely his own. Relax and laugh to the voice we know and love from Emperor's New Groove and Seinfeld!

Film_title: Other Worlds, Strange Dreams: The East German Space Operas of Director Gottfried Kolditz
Description: Video essay by film historian Evan Chester on Deaf Crocodile's release of "Signals - A Space Adventure/In The Dust of the Stars"
After his sister takes over his room during a party, a quiet photographer shares a bed with his roommate, forcing them to confront feelings they've been hiding.

Film_title: The Vienna Symphony Orchestra in Trieste Rossetti Theatre
Description: The Vienna Symphony Orchestra, conducted by its new conductor Petr Popelka, celebrates Johann Strauss II in the splendid setting of the Teatro Rossetti in Trieste.
One man goes through the same routine day by day with the responsibility of saving the world, but he will discover something that will question everything he's ever known.

Film_title: Golda Schultz, Rolando Villazón, and Ludovic Tézier @ Neuschwanstein Castle 2025
Description: The Deutsche Kammerphilharmonie Bremen, conducted by Omer Meir Wellber, takes over Neuschwanstein Castle in southern Germany for a lyrical concert featuring soprano Golda Schultz, tenor Rolando Villazón, and baritone Ludovic Tézier. In this grandiose building constructed by Ludwig II of Bavaria, the prestigious trio performs works by Mozart, Verdi, and Lehár.
3 Psycho Sadistic Serial Killer Clowns, are Released from Hell, After a Young Couple Decides to Play The Clownjuring, a Conjuring Spell Disguised as a Game.
Art Hain's Feature Documentary
Recently out of prison, John risks everything for a forbidden meeting with his ex and daughter, unveiling secrets, as their fragile connection teeters on the edge.

Joseph Zitt’s film dreamtext דברי חלום is a feature-length dream of coming to life in Israel. Four images of dancer Cheryl Nayowitz move past and through images of Israel and words spoken by the Holon Scratch Orchestra, along with music by the ensemble Gray Code. The film and its music are anchored by the song “Sachki Sachki,” sung by Vered Forbes. The film has no story, dialogue, or characters (other than the dancer). While there is text in Hebrew and English, you don’t need to know either language to enjoy the film. The English is a dreamlike text scrambled to approximate what you might hear while falling asleep. The Hebrew words which are heard and seen onscreen during much of the film are streams of consciousness, improvised by women’s voices on the themes of segments of the film. When we hear the song “Sachki Sachki,” the words appear onscreen both in the original Hebrew and in an English translation.

Film_title: Laurel & Hardy The Definitive Restorations, Volume 2
Description: Meticulously sourced from the original 35mm elements, this second volume continues the stellar work begun in the first set, offering fans eight beautifully restored short subjects that showcase Stan Laurel and Oliver Hardy at the height of their creative powers. The lineup offers up some rare restored versions of early sound comedy: Men O’ War (1929), Perfect Day (1929), Blotto (1930), Brats (1930), Another Fine Mess (1930), Dirty Work (1933), Going Bye-Bye! (1934), Them Thar Hills (1934), and Tit for Tat (1935). Each film gleams with newfound clarity, with picture and audio restoration that make even the most familiar routines feel fresh again.
In the year 1990 a police cadet searches for her estranged father who has gone missing in Yugoslavia, a country on the brink of civil war.
A girl's deal with a demonic entity takes an unexpectedly wholesome turn

Kyla sits through the quiet funeral of her late roommate, Elmo, with only three of her friends in attendance. She attempts to open up about his cause of death, but her well-meaning but nosy companions interrupt with elaborate and hilarious scenarios: Elmo as a mobster caught in a deadly game, a heartbroken lover killed in a telenovela-style betrayal, and even a secret superhero who perished saving an innocent. But when her friends' self-absorbed chatter leaves her alone again, the tragically absurd truth behind Elmo's... dirty demise is revealed.
It showcases the stories of how children are rebuilding their relationships with parents who were absent.

Baroque opera is a battlefield once more. Just as 17th-century Venice thrived on rivalry, dazzle and excess, today’s Early Music scene brims with competition. Into this arena, the Bayreuth Baroque Opera Festival — founded only in 2020 — has stormed with astonishing speed, staking its claim through neglected Italian gems and the intoxicating intimacy of the UNESCO-listed Margravial Opera House. No dry concepts here: Bayreuth trades in beauty, detail, and theatrical seduction. This year’s jewel, Cavalli’s Pompeo Magno (1666), became both a Venetian centrepiece and a specialist’s odyssey under conductor Leonardo García-Alarcón.

Film_title: Sometimes I Want To Return... Sensitive LIVE
Description: On the evening of February 12th, 2025, in a tiny venue deep in the heart of London’s West End, a very special group of people met to celebrate the launch of the album, Sensitive – An Indie Pop Anthology. This was a group of legendary, underground musicians who had helped define a movement characterised by an independent spirit, a desire to remain outside of the mainstream and to create romantic yet authentic music which spoke to everyday people about their everyday lives. The indie pop scene of the 1980’s was pivotal in shaping an attitude and aesthetic of do-it-yourself, the ripples of which are still felt today, over 40 years later, amongst young artists self-releasing music via digital platforms. Sensitive Live brought together the founding fathers of the indie pop movement for an exclusive, unforgettable, one-off show featuring performances of classic songs from the era, all of which can be heard in their original form on the Sensitive album.

Lita Ford is rock history personified. As guitarist for the legendary Runaways, she broke all the rules in the 1970s and paved the way for women in heavy rock. Her solo career produced true classics with hits like "Kiss Me Deadly" and the duet "Close My Eyes Forever." For decades, she has stood for uncompromising riffs, a rebellious attitude, and a voice that sounds like red-hot steel. Despite personal ups and downs, Lita has remained true to rock music and lives the lifestyle she sings about. When she takes the stage at Wacken, the hallowed ground gets its dose of pure rock power from a pioneer who still knows how to get an audience going.

Composed, written, and passionately performed by Ashoksaravanan and his team, Kaatrukku Traffic Signal illai "காற்றுக்கு டிராஃபிக் சிக்னல் இல்லை..." is a thunderous tribute to the bravery, sacrifice, and indomitable spirit of India’s soldiers. The song’s title metaphorically declares that just like the wind cannot be stopped by traffic signals, a warrior’s courage knows no limits. With powerful vocals, stirring lyrics, and an energetic composition, the song paints the life of a soldier as an epic saga of heroism—where the sky becomes their path, the earth their camp, and their uniform’s stars narrate untold tales of freedom. From the freezing Himalayas to the scorching deserts, the lyrics honor their unyielding vigilance, comparing them to eagles in the sky, tigers in battle, and volcanoes of unshakable resolve.

It speaks to the dreamers, the warriors, and the weary, reminding them that no struggle lasts forever and that courage can move mountains. Its message transcends boundaries: Keep fighting. The world bends when you refuse to break. Ashok Saravanan’s composition masterfully blends driving percussion, soaring vocals, and anthemic melodies to amplify the track’s fiery spirit. The powerful refrain—"When sorrow comes, don’t stop!"—echoes like a mantra, cementing its timeless call to perseverance.

The film presents gaps as institutional tools that reproduce exclusivity (after Sara Ahmed). Combining critical theory with narratives of marginalization and discrimination gathered through research interviews, the essay film offers an intersectional understanding of how whiteness is actively reproduced in our everyday environments. In addition to outlining the cultural-historical context of Finland, the film highlights the Academy of Fine Arts at the Uniarts Helsinki as a case study—examining the gap between its outwardly promoted policies and the reality of their implementation, as well as the institution’s exclusionary culture and discriminatory practices, drawing from the lived experiences of marginalized students.

TORN plunges into the battle that erupted on New York City's streets in the winter of 2023, after October 7, when the grassroots "KIDNAPPED" poster campaign-created to raise awareness about the 251 hostages taken by Hamas-became a lightning rod for rage and resistance. Through the voices of artists, activists, and hostage families, the film unravels how these posters turned subway walls and sidewalks into battlegrounds of ideology and emotion, igniting clashes between pro-Israel and pro-Palestinian New Yorkers. By exposing the motivations of those putting up and tearing down the posters, TORN reveals the complexities of this intense 'paper arm' proxy war, fought thousands of miles from the actual conflict-forcing a reckoning with identity, free speech, and empathy in an age of polarization.

Unearthed by James Balsamo That necromancer of the lens, that goblin king of gore comes a celluloid chimera so fried, so freaky, so fantastically confused, it can only be called Junior Webb's MYSTERIOUS WARRIOR. Behold: a CGI crab monster with a bad attitude, cardboard robot beatdowns in parking lot arenas, and southern-fried Kung Fu kicks that echo across the astral plane! This is a forgotten fever dream of fists, fireballs, and forbidden frocks a cinematic relic forged in the crusty crucible of camcorders, caffeine, and chaos.

Film_title: Poncho And Lefty in Chrome Don't Get Ya Home
Description: Lefty taunts Poncho’s dirty chopper, but his own pristine motorcycle won’t start. Overlooking basics, Lefty learns that looks don’t get ya home.
Two strangers find love, but are unfortunately separated. They reconnect on Christmas Eve five years later.

Ashoksaravanan's Nee Illa Tum Ninne Nuvvu is a groundbreaking multilingual breakup anthem that transcends linguistic boundaries, weaving together Tamil, English, Hindi, Malayalam, and Telugu into a soul-stirring musical tapestry. The song explores the raw, universal pain of lost love-capturing the emptiness, betrayal, and lingering sorrow that follows a shattered relationship. A rare fusion of South Indian melodies, Western grooves, and contemporary rhythms, the track blends Tamil soul, Hindi emotional depth, Telugu passion, Malayalam melancholy, and English lyrical vulnerability. Entirely self-created, Ashoksaravanan composes, produces, performs, and even handles the technical aspects-making it a true one-man musical revolution.—IMDb

Film_title: Don't Bring Your Man to Atlanta
Description: James and Leah pursue their dreams in Atlanta, but ambition pulls James into a dangerous nightlife. As Leah fights to maintain their love, betrayal emerges, sparking her transformation from heartbroken to vengeful, unraveling their lives.
A love that never really disappeared just delayed by a series of misunderstandings. If it were you, would you try to reach for it again?

Film_title: Little Dancer: A Musical in Concert
Description: She inspired a masterpiece… then vanished into the streets of 19th century Paris. Little Dancer is a new musical based on the story of Marie van Goethem, the girl who posed for impressionist Edgar Degas and became the heart of one of the most celebrated sculptures in history. This evocative original show is a bold exploration of the indomitable spirit of a young woman in a man’s world.

Film_title: Meeting the Lord During the Catastrophes (II)
Description: After Li Cheng'en accepts Almighty God's work of the last days, he hurries about preaching the gospel everywhere, gaining quite a few people. But then Li begins to exalt and showcase himself; just when he's feeling smug and self-satisfied, the newcomers he gained are misled and disturbed by a pastor, and Li is beaten by religious leaders and nearly freezes to death in the snow. He realizes that this is God's discipline and pruning coming upon him. Through reading God's words, he comes to know that his nature is too arrogant, that he doesn’t have a God-fearing heart, and that he is in dire need of God's judgment, chastisement, chastening, and discipline. After many years of experience, Li Cheng'en deeply understands that being able to welcome the Lord amid the great catastrophes, to experience God's judgment and cleansing while doing his duty, and to walk the path of salvation is God's immense grace and blessing to him …

Xi was molded early on for the role of leading a global superpower. But just how deeply he internalized Mao’s ideas only became clear over time, as authoritarian traits emerged that no one had quite anticipated – especially considering that Xi’s own father was a victim of the Cultural Revolution.

Premiered at the Maly Theatre of the Moscow Conservatory in 1879, 46 years after the novel’s publication, Eugene Onegin follows the story of a self-centered hero who dismisses love and accepts a duel with deadly consequences. However, while Pushkin’s verse is often sharp and satirical, Tchaikovsky’s music emphasizes the characters’ strength of feeling. Join a brilliant lineup — including Ukrainian baritone Iurii Samoilov as Onegin and soprano Kristina Mkhitaryan as Tatyana — alongside the Chorus and Orchestra of the Teatro Real, led by conductor Gustavo Gimeno, in this "profound and intimate exploration of love, regret, and honor" (OperaWire).

M.A. Littler's film diary, Beyond the White Rains, subtly reflects the dichotomy of man, mind, and place. In Littler's singular style, the film juxtaposes an author's interior landscape with the dramatic exterior of a remote coastal setting. Set within the interior of an old whaler's cottage, the film captures the ever-changing weather, the impervious sea, and moving clouds, reflecting a lifelong study of the interaction between man and place. Enigmatic, elegiac, and at times eerie, the film's pared-down images and otherworldly sound design reveal both the external beauty and the haunting interior landscape.

Film_title: Made in New Jersey: Films From Fort Lee
Description: Fort Lee was already a favorite location for New York filmmakers when Mark Dintenfass arrived in 1910 and established Champion as the first of many studios in that sleepy town. Biograph, Éclair, Kalem, Fox and Universal soon followed making Fort Lee and New Jersey the birthplace of the American motion picture industry. Curated by Richard Koszarski, author of Fort Lee, The Film Town and the enclosed 20-page booklet, this special edition 2-disc Blu-ray set gathers 14 wonderful and historically significant films and 2 documentaries from archives around the world. Featuring two restored Biograph chase films by D.W. Griffith (The Curtain Pole and The Cord of Life), the legendary The Vampire (1913), which popularized the character of the “vamp” or “femme fatale,” the earliest surviving film version of Robin Hood, and a new 4K restoration of Edgar G. Ulmer’s Ukrainian operetta Cossacks in Exile (1939) famously shot at Little Flower Monastery in Newton

Film_title: The Home In Which I Tried This
Description: 400+ repurposed screenshots sourced from the Jackass TV show removed from context and void of people. The images are arranged into a montage of hazy, digital noise oscillating between form and abstraction. The images function as non-spaces; liminal conduits which serve no purpose but transition between focus. The video drudges at a glacial pace, images distort and amalgamate into one another through staggered crossfades, a humming visual dirge aglow. A digital spectre haunts the language of memory; the ubiquity of cultural landmarks like MTV would be echoed through the early digital spaces. Programs like Jackass would not only be replicated physically, but so too the videos themselves - through perpetual iteration, compression artifacts and digital distortion would increasingly become indivisible from the image of culture. The aesthetics of virality and memetics were tethered to the digital, visual memory indivisible from the screen-based contexts which perpetuated it.

"Unmaikku Udharanam En Magan" (My Son is the Example of Truth) is not merely a song but a historic artifact in the evolution of Tamil music. [world's first Ai Tamil Album] As the first officially registered and copyright-protected Human-AI collaboration, it dismantles the boundaries between organic creativity and technological innovation. The project is helmed by the visionary Ashoksaravanan, who, along with his team, composed the music and penned the deeply personal lyrics. This song stands as a legally protected benchmark, proudly claiming its title and paving the way for a new genre of collaborative art in the Tamil industry. For this Song Ashoksaravanan has been conferred the prestigious Bharat Ratna Iconic Achievers Award 2025 in recognition of his exceptional contributions to the fields of music and lyricism. This honor was presented by Jeevan Jagriti Seva Sansthan, an NGO whose "Bharat Ratna Iconic Achievers Award" is affiliated with the Government of India and NITI Aayog.
